text: <par>The original gloss, in Chinese, is <latinform>hǔkǒu</latinform> (lit. "tiger’s mouth"), ‘part of hand between the thumb and index finger.’ </par>
<par>1381? (What is (shoumai)?)(Chinese: shou = 'hand'; mai = 'pulse'; thus technically this refers to the pulse taken at the wrist ... ZJH)</par>
<par>The second syllable has been assigned to etymon 558 *g-ram despite the heterorganic final consonant; the first syllable, <latinform>ʐau³¹</latinform>, is ‘clf. for elongated objects’ .</par>
<par>Is this the direct cognate? Or is 17525 <latinform>ʂa⁵⁵</latinform> ‘gasp, pant (chuan)’ ?</par>
